nativeRange.habitat: Diverse lowland habitats including streams, rivers, paddy fields, swamps, wells, reservoirs, and brackish coastal margins; a generalist surface-dweller tolerating a wide range of conditions

diet.notes: A surface-oriented predator that has been documented readily accepting frozen meaty foods such as bloodworms, brine shrimp, daphnia, and tubifex, as well as quality pellets or flake. Small live insects such as fruit flies and mosquito larvae are taken eagerly and are reported to enhance color and conditioning. This species tends to consume any small fish or invertebrate that fits in its mouth — neon tetras, ember tetras, dwarf shrimp, and similar nano species are predation risks.

goodWith: medium-bodied tetras (e.g. Congo tetra, Buenos Aires tetra)
avoidWith: small nano fish (neon tetras, ember tetras, chili rasboras, microrasboras — predation risk)
dwarf shrimp (cherry shrimp, amano shrimp — will be eaten)
other male Aplocheilus lineatus in small tanks (conspecific aggression)

imageCandidates.notes: Marrabbio2; 2332x1144. Sharp, vibrant full-body male in classic gold/yellow 'Golden Wonder' colour with red gill spot, upturned surface-feeding mouth and rear-set yellow dorsal — textbook diagnostic shot.
Uploader 'golden panchax'; 1652x1239. Sharp pair cruising at the surface showing iridescent gold-green flanks and red-tipped fins; good group/surface-behaviour representation.
Same uploader; 4128x3096. High-res pair at the surface of a planted tank, crisp iridescent gold-green colour; different angle for variety.

summary: The Golden Wonder Killifish is a captive selectively-bred gold color form of the Striped Panchax (Aplocheilus lineatus), a bold surface predator native to India and Sri Lanka. Adults reach up to 10 cm and will readily eat small fish or shrimp. Unlike many killifish, this species is non-annual and lives 3–5 years in captivity.
careNotes:
Non-annual killifish: unlike the well-known Nothobranchius annual killifish, Aplocheilus lineatus inhabits permanent water bodies and does not require a dry-season diapause (a dormant period spent in dried-out substrate). Captive lifespans of 3–5 years are commonly reported.

Size and predation: at up to 10 cm, this is one of the larger killifish commonly available in the trade — considerably larger than Aphyosemion or Fundulopanchax species. It tends to consume any fish or invertebrate small enough to fit in its mouth. Small tankmates such as neon tetras, ember tetras, chili rasboras, and dwarf shrimp are at risk and should generally be avoided.

Gold form vs. wild form: the 'Golden Wonder' trade name refers to a captive selectively-bred gold/yellow color variant of Aplocheilus lineatus. The wild form is silver-green with yellow stripes. Both share identical care requirements. The gold form is the most commonly sold variety in the aquarium trade.

Escape risk — tight lid mandatory: Aplocheilus lineatus is considered a confirmed jumper and has been widely documented exiting aquariums through gaps. A tight-fitting lid is generally considered essential, not optional. Lowering the water surface 5–10 cm below the rim is commonly recommended as an additional precaution.

Surface focus: this species is primarily a top-to-upper-water-column dweller, spending most of its time near the surface. Floating plants and surface cover are reported to reduce stress and are consistent with the species' natural habitat (vegetated stream margins, paddy field edges).

Semi-aggressive: males tend to be territorial and may show aggression toward each other in confined spaces. A ratio of 1 male to 1–2 females is generally advised for a pair or trio setup. With sufficient space and visual breaks, multiple males can sometimes coexist, though close monitoring is recommended.

Water chemistry: a relatively adaptable species documented tolerating a wide range of conditions in both freshwater and occasional brackish margins in the wild. Brackish tolerance has been recorded in the species' native range; pure freshwater is the standard aquarium recommendation. The species is not considered euryhaline for aquarium purposes — standard freshwater husbandry applies.

Temperature note: some sources cite a tighter range of 22–25°C, while several hobbyist sources extend this to 28°C. The broader 22–28°C range is consistent with the species' wide natural distribution. Values at the cooler end (22–24°C) may be associated with better health and longevity in practice.

Conspecific aggression note: males have been known to fight and should be monitored in tanks under 120L. In smaller tanks, a single male with one or two females is the commonly advised approach.
breedingNotes: Generally considered relatively straightforward to breed. Eggs are deposited individually among fine-leaved plants, spawning mops, or plant roots near the surface over a period of days to weeks. Eggs are typically adhesive and attach to the spawning medium. Incubation at 24–26°C is commonly reported to take approximately 10–14 days. Adults have been known to consume eggs and fry; removing eggs to a separate container or using a spawning mop that can be transferred is generally recommended. Fry are relatively large at hatching and have been documented accepting newly hatched brine shrimp nauplii and micro-worms from first feeding. Unlike annual killifish, eggs do not require a dry-period diapause — continuous water incubation is standard.

primary.name: Aquatic Arts
primary.notes: Used as primary after Aquarium Co-Op returned 404 for a dedicated golden-wonder page, and SeriouslyFish had no species page for Aplocheilus lineatus. Aquatic Arts is a reputable specialist retailer; confirmed adult size (3–4 in / 7.5–10 cm), temperature (22–25°C), pH (6.0–7.5), kH (3–8 dKH), swim zone (top), escape risk (confirmed jumper), diet notes, and tankmate guidance.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name, family (Aplocheilidae), order (Cyprinodontiformes), native range (Peninsular India, Sri Lanka), maximum adult size (10 cm TL), and habitat description (streams, paddy fields, swamps, brackish margins). FishBase temperature range 22–25°C; FishkeepingWorld/AquaWorldHub extend to 28°C — broader range retained. Minimum tank length 80 cm confirmed.

additional.name: AquaWorldHub
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (22–28°C), pH (6.0–7.5), gH (5–15 dGH), tank size (20 gallons / 76L minimum for a group), diet (omnivorous; flake, pellets, live/frozen), and compatible tankmates. Used to confirm broader temperature range and gH. Note: this source does not classify the species as non-annual; non-annual status is confirmed from FishBase habitat data (permanent water bodies) and controller brief.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op — Top 5 Killifish
additional.notes: Confirmed adult size (3–4 inches / 7.5–10 cm), hardiness, predatory nature toward small fish and dwarf shrimp, and general positioning as the largest commonly available killifish. Limited parameter specifics for this species individually.

fish.breedingNotes: Deposits adhesive eggs among fine-leaved plants or spawning mops near the surface; eggs hatch in roughly 10–14 days. Adults should be separated from eggs to prevent predation. Fry are large enough at hatching to accept newly hatched brine shrimp nauplii from first feeding. See breedingNotes for full details.
